macro_summary: Tuesday's session saw the S&P 500 dip 0.7% to 733.73 as 10-year Treasury yields surged to 4.67% — a 15-month high — after hot April CPI and PPI prints forced bond markets to price in potential Fed rate hikes by year-end, per the CME FedWatch Tool. Oil swung wildly, with WTI settling at $103.81 after Trump postponed an Iran strike, while the US sanctioned 19 vessels, keeping Hormuz supply risks alive. NVDA earnings loom tomorrow at $220.61, with the semis cluster eking out a flat close despite the macro selloff.
commodities_note: WTI held above $103, reflecting ongoing fears of new US-Iran combat amid a 10-vessel-per-day Hormuz trickle (per Kpler). Gold edged up 0.25% to $4,474.5, while the 30-year Treasury yield broke above 5.14% — a level not seen since 2007 — as inflation expectations unseat the earlier rate-cut narrative.

singapore.summary: All three banks are CAUTION with extension blocks of 3-8% as US10Y rises further above 4.20%, deepening the dual NIM squeeze. UOB is the least extended (+3.1%) and saw RSI improve, but volume warns; OCBC’s relative outperformance is fading with RSI still elevated.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally impaired — NIM compression from dual channel intact; no relief until US10Y sustainably breaks 4.20% or MAS eases in October.
singapore.key_development: Singapore DPM urged financial firms to use AI for job creation (per Reuters, May 20), but the rate-driven NIM drag remains the dominant near-term negative.

semiconductors.summary: All 11 semis names are CAUTION, dominated by extreme extension: 6 are >20% above their 50-day, another 2 >10%. Only ASML sits near the 50-day with a slightly improved R:R. TSEM’s +17.8% weekly surge on photonics deals highlights the speculative froth.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically overextended — AI capex cycle intact but most names need a 15-30% pullback to offer acceptable risk/reward.
semiconductors.key_development: OpenAI’s guaranteed compute capacity offering (per CNBC) could boost NVDA/TSM demand, but NVDA earnings tomorrow will be the single biggest cluster catalyst.

big_tech.summary: The BigTech cluster remains CAUTION across AMZN, GOOG, MSFT, and PLTR as rates pressure multiples. GOOG’s +15% extension is the hardest block, while MSFT’s pullback to +4.5% above the 50-day makes it the closest to a re-entry zone, though OpenAI credit circularity caps enthusiasm.
big_tech.thesis_status: Structurally bullish — cloud and AI revenue growth intact, but valuation reset by rising rates. No rush to enter until yields stabilize.
big_tech.key_development: OpenAI’s guaranteed compute offering (per CNBC, May 19) directly benefits MSFT Azure, but also raises capex sustainability questions for hyperscalers.

interconnected.reason: Snapdragon AI edge chips leverage Arm architecture — on-device AI inference play tied to NVDA’s data-center buildout story.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if QCOM pulls back 8-10% from current levels after NVDA earnings, confirming edge inference as the next AI wave.

interconnected.reason: Received matching $2B NVDA photonics investment; LITE’s partner and direct beneficiary of AI optics buildout.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after LITE’s next 50-day retest near $830 — if LITE stabilizes, COHR becomes attractive on a similar pullback.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: Hyperscaler networking switch leader — benefits from MSFT/GOOG AI capex directly as they expand data-center fabric.
interconnected.entry_note: Attractive if MSFT reclaims $425 and GOOG breaks above $404 — then Arista likely leads networking.

active_risks: US-Iran conflict keeps Strait of Hormuz partially closed; 19 vessels sanctioned by US Treasury, oil markets volatile with WTI $100-104.
Fed’s Warsh inherits accelerating CPI (3.8% headline) and PPI (6% YoY); bond market now pricing in rate hikes; 10Y hits 4.67%, 30Y above 5.14%.
US-China tariff deal stalling: Trump-Xi summit yields no chip export control discussion; China’s April retail sales flat, industrial production decelerates sharply.
Semiconductor supply chain pressures from Iran war increase chemical/gas costs, flagged by TSMC, Foxconn, Infineon (per CNBC, May 19).
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil near $104 and Hormuz risk keeps energy costs elevated, hitting consumer discretionary and logistics. Tech multiples compress under rising yields, with NVDA earnings tomorrow the near-term pivot for semis. Singapore banks suffer dual NIM compression from rates and strong SGD.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: US Treasury sanctioned 19 vessels and an Iranian shadow banking network. Trump postponed Iran strike Monday, but combat fears resurfaced Tuesday.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: No new entries as VIX hovers near 18 and yields surge; wait for NVDA earnings to settle the semis outlook before deploying capital.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Defensive — technical extension and rate headwinds dominate
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 13 of 22 names are hard-blocked on technical extension, leaving zero BUY/ACCUMULATE signals; the portfolio is in full wait-and-watch mode ahead of NVDA earnings and a potential Fed pivot signal.
